name: AIQA TOOL - AI Bug Triage and Root Cause Analyzer
description: Analyzes failed tests, logs, screenshots, and network notes to classify likely root cause and owner. Uses a prompt template plus configurable chat model. OpenAI-compatible providers can use OpenAI, Groq, OpenRouter, Ollama, or LM Studio base URLs. Anthropic users can swap the chat model component in LangFlow.

providerSupport: LM Studio via local OpenAI-compatible endpoint
Anthropic by replacing the chat model node

sampleInput.logs: Expected status 200 but received 500 on POST /checkout. Stack trace shows PaymentGatewayTimeout.

data.label: AI Bug Triage and Root Cause Analyzer Input

data.template:
You are a bug triage specialist. Use the provided evidence to classify probable root cause and next steps. Distinguish product defects from test issues.

Project: AI Bug Triage and Root Cause Analyzer
Objective: Cut triage time by clustering failures and creating actionable bug investigation summaries.
Primary use case: A CI pipeline sends failure logs. The tool returns likely root cause, owner, severity, duplicate hints, and next debug steps.

Required task:
Analyze the failure context. Provide root cause category, evidence, owner suggestion, severity, confidence, immediate debug steps, and a concise Jira bug draft.

Required output:
- Start with an executive summary.
- Include assumptions and missing information.
- Provide concrete QA artifacts that can be copied into a real workflow.
- Include an implementation checklist.
- Return Markdown first, then a compact JSON summary.

User input:
{project_input}

data.openai_api_base_note: Set base URL for Groq, OpenRouter, Ollama, or LM Studio when using OpenAI-compatible mode.

data.label: AI Bug Triage and Root Cause Analyzer Output
